patient_description: Honestly, I feel like my entire body is running in slow motion, and I just can't shake this overwhelming exhaustion. For the last few months, I’ve been completely drained, even if I manage to sleep for nine or ten hours. I've also put on about fifteen pounds out of nowhere, which makes no sense because my appetite has actually decreased and my stomach constantly feels backed up and bloated. I am also absolutely freezing all the time. I'm wearing wool socks and heavy sweaters inside the house while my partner is comfortable in a t-shirt. My skin has become incredibly dry, rough, and itchy, no matter how much lotion I slather on. Lately, when I look in the mirror, my face—especially around my eyes—looks swollen and puffy, almost like I'm retaining water there. On top of all that, my legs and arms feel heavy and weak, to the point where just climbing a flight of stairs feels like a major athletic event. I just feel sluggish from head to toe.
doctor_description: Subjective: A 42-year-old female presents with a four-month history of progressive, generalized lethargy and profound fatigue that is unresponsive to rest. She reports an unintentional weight gain of approximately 6.8 kg (15 lbs) over this period, despite reporting a reduced caloric intake due to persistent, moderate-to-severe constipation. The patient notes severe cold intolerance, stating she requires heavy layering indoors. She also reports generalized muscle weakness, particularly in her proximal lower extremities, and has noticed increasingly dry, rough skin and facial puffiness. Objective: * Vital Signs: Blood pressure 112/74 mmHg, temperature 36.1°C (97.0°F), respiratory rate 12 breaths/min. Notable bradycardia is present with a resting heart rate of 52 beats per minute. * General Appearance: Patient appears fatigued with a flat affect and slowed speech. Mild periorbital puffiness and generalized non-pitting facial edema (myxedema) are observed. * Dermatological: Skin is cold to the touch, coarse, extremely dry, and showing signs of mild scaling on the bilateral extensor surfaces of the forearms and shins. * Musculoskeletal/Neurological: Mild, symmetric proximal muscle weakness (4/5) noted in the quadriceps bilaterally. Deep tendon reflexes (Achilles) exhibit a delayed relaxation phase. Chest is clear to auscultation, and abdomen is mildly distended with hypoactive bowel sounds.
